title: Cut ideas for the final scene
display_title: Cut ideas for the final scene
wikitext:
[]: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sun Feb 02, 2025 2:19 am tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) We need a proper translation, but basically Anno and Tsurumaki mentioned earlier today on an event for the new Gundam show an earlier idea for the final scene where Mari was on the other side of the train station. DeepL wrote: Director Kazuya Tsurumaki has said that recently Director Anno has been liberated from Eva and is in full otaku mode, just like he was with Top and Nadia, and is really into it #ジークアクス #GQuuuuuuX

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Sun Feb 02, 2025 7:36 am DeepL wrote: Director Kazuya Tsurumaki has said that recently Director Anno has been liberated from Eva and is in full otaku mode, just like he was with Top and Nadia, and is really into it #ジークアクス #GQuuuuuuX

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by DTGee » Sun Feb 02, 2025 11:11 pm This is referring to the scene in 2.0 when Shinji and Mari first meet, not the final scene of 3.0+1.0.

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by Konja7 » Mon Feb 03, 2025 5:55 am FelipeFritschF wrote: I'm not sure they mean that Mari would just be another bystander (no mention of Rei, Kaworu or Asuka), if Mari and Shinji would meet by chance, if Mari crossed to the othr side of the station (or if Shinji would do so). Mari showing up out of "nowhere" of course is indeed a callback to her parachuting in. Presumably this will be talked about more in the upcoming CRC interviews. It might also be referring to Mari's introduction in 2.0? An interesting note nonetheless.

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 04, 2025 12:06 pm Weird_ocean wrote: DeepL#944448 wrote: Director Kazuya Tsurumaki has said that recently Director Anno has been liberated from Eva and is in full otaku mode, just like he was with Top and Nadia, and is really into it #ジークアクス #GQuuuuuuX

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Fri Feb 07, 2025 7:51 pm Weird_ocean wrote: DeepL#944448 wrote: Director Kazuya Tsurumaki has said that recently Director Anno has been liberated from Eva and is in full otaku mode, just like he was with Top and Nadia, and is really into it #ジークアクス #GQuuuuuuX

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by Joseki » Sat Feb 08, 2025 2:42 pm In the context of 2.0, everything new to Shinji's life drop from the sky (Asuka, Mari, Kaworu) and there is a thematic continuity in that, however I think Mari and Shinji meeting on the train station platform would have ended up being a more interesting encounter in light of how the story ended 2 movies and 12 years later.

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by jakasspeech1 » Tue Mar 18, 2025 4:36 am Weird_ocean wrote: That kind of confirms my suspicion that Anno didn't actually want to work on Shin and was tired of EVA.

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Mar 18, 2025 6:41 am jakasspeech1 wrote: I agree!

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Mar 18, 2025 5:18 pm kuribo-04 wrote: Like he would have rushed it in some way I imagine.

[]: Re: Cut ideas for the final scene             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 18, 2025 5:33 pm Konja7 wrote: I think they mean Shinji and Mari meeting in 2.0. That's why they mentioned the encounter ended up with Shinji bumping into Mari as she came down by parachute. It's pretty interesting they originally considered Shinji and Mari encounter in 2.0 to be in the train station. That place has always been full of symbolism for Shinji.
